query: What is the unemployment rate for rural males in Bihar during 2022-23?

response_full:
According to the Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS) 2022–23 by the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI):

Unemployment Rate (UR) for rural males in Bihar (age 15 years and above)

Status: PS+SS (Usual Status – Principal + Subsidiary)

Year: 2022–23

➡️ Unemployment Rate: 4.4%

Interpretation

This means 4.4% of the rural male labour force in Bihar was unemployed during 2022–23.

“Unemployed” refers to persons not working but seeking or available for work under the usual status (principal + subsidiary) measure.

Key details

State: Bihar

Sector: Rural

Gender: Male

Age group: 15 years and above

Measure: Usual Status (PS+SS)

✅ So, the rural male unemployment rate in Bihar in 2022–23 was 4.4%.

response_full:
According to PLFS (Periodic Labour Force Survey) 2023–24, the Worker Population Ratio (WPR) for Scheduled Tribes (ST) in Rajasthan is:

📊 Worker Population Ratio (WPR) – Scheduled Tribes

State: Rajasthan

Year: 2023–24

Social Category: Scheduled Tribe

WPR: ≈ 49–50 % (persons, age 15 years and above)

🔎 Meaning

WPR = Percentage of the population that is working (employed).

A ~50 % WPR means about half of the ST population aged 15+ in Rajasthan is employed.

📌 Interpretation

ST communities typically show higher WPR than many other social groups because:

Greater participation in agriculture and informal work

Higher family labour participation

Lower rates of higher education continuation

🧾 Example (approximate comparison)
Social Group	WPR Rajasthan (2023-24)
Scheduled Tribe	~49–50 %
Scheduled Caste	~44–46 %
OBC	~46–48 %
Others	~43–45 %

If you want, I can also give:

📍 Male vs Female WPR for ST in Rajasthan (2023-24)

📍 Rural vs Urban WPR for ST

📍 Trend of ST WPR in Rajasthan from 2017-18 to 2023-24 (very useful for exams/research).
